value.found_quote: Each skill is a folder with a SKILL.md file plus any supporting files it needs. By default, both you and Claude can invoke a skill. Use frontmatter to control that: disable-model-invocation: true for user-only workflows like /deploy, or user-invocable: false to hide from the / menu while Claude can still invoke it.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes a "skills/" directory feature in Claude Code where each skill is a named folder containing a SKILL.md file plus supporting files, invocable via /skill-name by both users and Claude. The page confirms: (1) reusable, named prompt bundles — skills are named directories with SKILL.md; (2) packaging multi-step workflows with supporting files — "bundle reference docs, templates, scripts" alongside SKILL.md; (3) both user and Claude can invoke by name — "by default, both you and Claude can invoke a skill"; (4) support for spawning subagents — the page also describes an "agents/" folder for subagents that Claude can spawn. The page also notes commands and skills are "now the same mechanism," with skills being the preferred approach. The feature appears to be currently active/generally available.
